english: From the forest of his matted locks flows the pure stream of Ganga, purifying his neck
On whose neck hangs a long garland of great serpents
The resounding beat of the Damaru drum — dam dam dam dam — resounds

hindi: चकार चण्डताण्डवं तनोतु नः शिवः शिवम्॥
english: Shiva performs the fierce Tandava dance — may he bestow auspiciousness on us
roman: Chakara chandatandavam tanotu nah shivah shivam

english: From the whirling, churning matted locks the divine Ganga flows
Her waves sport playfully on the magnificent crown of Shiva
On the forehead blazes the sacred fire — dhagad dhagad dhagaj
May my delight every moment be in Shiva who bears the crescent moon on his crest
Shiva, the dear companion in the sport of Parvati daughter of the mountain king
Whose mind is ever rejoicing, spreading joy to all directions
Whose gracious sidelong glance averts all fierce misfortunes
May my mind find delight in the sky-clad Lord Shiva
The reddish-brown serpent on his matted locks radiates the lustre of its jewelled hood
The cardinal directions are reddened as if smeared with the saffron of Kadamba flowers
His body resplendent, draped with the skin of a majestic intoxicated elephant
May my mind find wondrous delight in the Lord who sustains all beings
He is honored by Indra with a thousand eyes and all the divine hosts
The footstool of his feet is silvered with the pollen-dust showered by the gods
His matted locks are bound by the garland of the king of serpents
May the Lord with the moon as his crown bestow lasting glory
